ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for bandwidth utilization: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Support read, validate, mutate, rollback, and audit workflows for bandwidth utilization with disaster-recovery guardrails.
backend_scope: Add validated commands, dry-run support, RBAC checks, and audit records for bandwidth utilization.
frontend_scope: Add action menu, confirmations, diff previews, and result status for bandwidth utilization.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Network Observability bandwidth utilization operations with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: bandwidth-utilization_disaster-recovery_operations evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for bandwidth utilization disaster-recovery that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for bandwidth utilization; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for bandwidth utilization.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for bandwidth utilization recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for bandwidth utilization.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for bandwidth utilization.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing bandwidth utilization data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling bandwidth utilization operations broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for bandwidth utilization.
acceptance_criteria: Given bandwidth utilization data exists, when the disaster-recovery operations workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: bandwidth utilization connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for network cost attribution: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Create repeatable runbooks and scheduled checks for network cost attribution resilience posture.
backend_scope: Add runbook schema, scheduler hooks, history, and notification events for network cost attribution.
frontend_scope: Add runbook editor, schedule controls, history table, and owner assignment for network cost attribution.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Network Observability network cost attribution automation with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: network-cost-attribution_resilience_automation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for network cost attribution resilience that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for network cost attribution; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for network cost attribution.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for network cost attribution recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for network cost attribution.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for network cost attribution.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing network cost attribution data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling network cost attribution automation broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for network cost attribution.
acceptance_criteria: Given network cost attribution data exists, when the resilience automation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: network cost attribution connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for route table analysis: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Support read, validate, mutate, rollback, and audit workflows for route table analysis with security guardrails.
backend_scope: Add validated commands, dry-run support, RBAC checks, and audit records for route table analysis.
frontend_scope: Add action menu, confirmations, diff previews, and result status for route table analysis.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Network Observability route table analysis operations with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: route-table-analysis_security_operations evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for route table analysis security that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for route table analysis; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for route table analysis.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for route table analysis recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for route table analysis.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for route table analysis.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing route table analysis data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling route table analysis operations broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for route table analysis.
acceptance_criteria: Given route table analysis data exists, when the security operations workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: route table analysis connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for network path root cause: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Support read, validate, mutate, rollback, and audit workflows for network path root cause with cost guardrails.
backend_scope: Add validated commands, dry-run support, RBAC checks, and audit records for network path root cause.
frontend_scope: Add action menu, confirmations, diff previews, and result status for network path root cause.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Network Observability network path root cause operations with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: network-path-root-cause_cost_operations evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for network path root cause cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for network path root cause; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for network path root cause.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for network path root cause, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for network path root cause.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for network path root cause.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing network path root cause data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling network path root cause operations broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for network path root cause.
acceptance_criteria: Given network path root cause data exists, when the cost operations workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: network path root cause connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
